title: Present Tense
shortSummary: Master the present tense — the most important tense in Spanish. Learn to conjugate regular -ar, -er, and -ir verbs, plus the essential irregular verbs ser, estar, tener, and ir. This unlocks the ability to talk about what you do, what is happening now, and your daily routine.
whyItMatters: The present tense is the backbone of Spanish communication. It covers what you do habitually, what is happening right now, and even near-future plans. Master this and you can hold basic conversations about almost any topic.
coreConcepts: Ser vs estar — the two 'to be' verbs
Tener (to have) and its idiomatic uses
Ir (to go) and ir + a + infinitive for future plans
Common irregular verbs: querer, poder, saber, conocer
